summary: Reporter asks whether SkiaSharp can be configured to throw .NET-style exceptions on failure instead of returning boolean false, and whether extended error details can be retrieved when operations fail.

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Evaluating SkiaSharp as replacement for GDI+/System.Drawing.dll

repoLinks.description: Closely related follow-up question (#1169) asking same thing — closed by maintainer as completed in 2020

analysis.summary: The reporter is confused by SkiaSharp's C-style boolean error reporting rather than .NET-style exception throwing. The maintainer has already answered this in comments: exception-throwing is a design constraint because (a) SkiaSharp cannot make breaking changes, (b) a duplicate API is not viable, and (c) Skia's native layer often does not provide extended error information. This is by-design behaviour, not a bug.
analysis.rationale: The issue is a usage question with a clear answer that has already been provided by the maintainer. `SKBitmap.ScalePixels` returns bool as confirmed by code inspection. The pattern is by-design, matching the underlying Skia C API. Related issue #1169 asks the same question and was formally closed as completed. The appropriate action is to provide a summarising answer and close.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKBitmap.ScalePixels returns bool reflecting the native Skia API return value; null checks throw ArgumentNullException but operation failure returns false with no extended error information
Constructors DO throw InvalidOperationException when the native handle is null, establishing a mixed pattern: constructors throw, operations return bool

keySignals.text: Methods that can fail seem to return false only, like e.g. SKBitmap.ScalePixels
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter has correctly identified the boolean-return error handling pattern in SkiaSharp

keySignals.text: we can't do breaking changes ... a duplicate API is not really viable ... starting with exceptions now will be very confusing/unexpected
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer confirmed this is by-design and cannot be changed due to ABI and API stability constraints

keySignals.text: Often there is no actual data coming from the native API, so this is hard. However, some APIs also have an overload that provides some result or status value.
keySignals.interpretation: Partial answer: some APIs do provide richer feedback via overloads with result/status output parameters

workarounds: Check the bool return value and throw your own exception: `if (!bitmap.ScalePixels(dst, options)) throw new InvalidOperationException("ScalePixels failed");`
Validate inputs before calling (dimensions, color types compatible) to reduce unexpected false returns
Some SKPixmap/SKImage APIs expose overloads or related methods that return status values with more context

resolution.hypothesis: The reporter wants .NET-style exception throwing. This is a known by-design limitation of SkiaSharp's thin wrapper over Skia's C API. The workaround is to check return values and throw application-level exceptions as needed.

proposals.title: Wrap boolean return in your own exception-throwing helper
proposals.description: Callers can trivially create extension methods or helpers that throw on false, giving them .NET-style error handling at the application layer.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Wrap boolean return in your own exception-throwing helper
resolution.recommendedReason: Simple, ABI-safe, and gives the caller full control over the exception type and message.

actionability.reason: The behaviour is by-design: SkiaSharp follows the Skia C API convention of returning bool/null on failure. The maintainer has already explained this in comments. Related issue #1169 was closed as completed for the same question.

actions.description: Post answer explaining the by-design pattern and providing workaround
Close as answered — behaviour is by-design and workaround provided
Cross-reference related question #1169 which covers the same topic
